headline: Putrajaya Wetlands Lose 35% Bird Species in 15 Years
context: MNS Putrajaya's 2025 bird census recorded 124 species, down from 191 species in 2010, with 67 species no longer present at the constructed wetland park.

cards.big: Putrajaya Wetlands was Malaysia's showcase constructed wetland.
cards.sub: 67 bird species have disappeared in 15 years. The showcase is failing.

cards.big: Water quality in the wetlands dropped from Class II to Class III since 2018.
cards.sub: Stormwater runoff from 8 new developments carries sediment and nutrients into the wetland system.

cards.big: Wetland buffer zones were reduced from 100m to 30m for 3 adjacent developments.
cards.sub: Planning approvals overrode the original wetland management plan.

cards.big: Constructed wetlands require RM2.4M annual maintenance for ecological function.
cards.sub: Actual maintenance budget has averaged RM0.8M — one-third of the requirement.

cards.big: Building a wetland and then underfunding it is building a photo opportunity, not an ecosystem.
Constructed wetlands are infrastructure. Like roads and bridges, they need maintenance budgets. Without them, they degrade — and the investment is wasted.
